mission.title: envoy: Feature Request: Support adding/removing query params
mission.description: Feature Request: Support adding/removing query params. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```
In Nginx you can modify query params with something like `set $args $args&api_key=foo;`

We can do something similar with `query_params_to_add` and `query_params_to_remove`

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature Request: Support adding/removing query params
steps.description:
I don't have a strong opinion. I think it's fine to build it in as I think that is what most people would expect. There shouldn't be any overhead of not used I don't think. Thanks for implementing!
```yaml
http_filters:
  - name: envoy.filters.http.lua
    typed_config:
      '@type': type.googleapis.com/envoy.config.filter.http.lua.v2.Lua
      inline_code: |
        function envoy_on_request(request_handle)
          path = request_handle:headers():get(":path")
          request_handle:headers():replace(":path", (path .. "&my-param=yeet"))
        end
  - name: envoy.router
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feature Request: Support adding/removing query params" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I don't have a strong opinion. I think it's fine to build it in as I think that is what most people would expect. There shouldn't be any overhead of not used I don't think. Thanks for implementing!

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
